title: agapanthus campanulatus subsp. patens
intro: Agapanthus campanulatus subsp. patens, also known as the African lily or blue lily, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Agapanthus genus and is native to South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Agapanthus campanulatus subsp. patens include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat various ailments, such as digestive issues and skin conditions.
